question: v1 forensics vocabulary: the proposed six metrics (step/tool distribution, edit-test cadence, thrash rate, time-to-first-test, error-recovery latency, destructive-command count) and four detectors (holdout-tamper, hardcoded-expected-output, test-skip insertion, suspicious single-step completion)?
author: claude/design

question: LLM forensic review pass: in v1 (reusing EVAL-9's blinded, isolated, kappa-calibrated pattern) or deferred to a follow-up slice, shipping the deterministic tier alone first?
author: claude/design

question: Gaming-flag disposition: disclose-only plus a ledgered operator quarantine path, or auto-fail/auto-quarantine flagged trials?
author: claude/design

question: Fence coupling: should any forensic flag refuse the official render in v1, or is forensics disclosure-only until detector precision is established via calibration?
author: claude/design

rationale: Decision session 2026-07-04: all EVAL-10/11/12 recommendations accepted. Six metrics + four detectors as specced; the vocabulary is closed and versioned so additions are deliberate version bumps, not drift
author: jyang

rationale: Accepted 2026-07-04. EVAL-9's blinded/isolated/kappa-calibrated pattern already exists to reuse; shipping the LLM pass alongside the deterministic tier lets calibration data accumulate from the first experiment
author: jyang

rationale: Accepted 2026-07-04. Flags are evidence, never verdicts: auto-failing on an unproven detector would bias the instrument it protects; the operator path is ledgered so no disposition is invisible
author: jyang

rationale: Accepted 2026-07-04. A detector must prove its precision through calibration before it can block an official finding; fence coupling is revisited as a new decision once kappa data exists
author: jyang
